t_ch: 只要一个具备时尚感，有成为演员、歌手潜力的素人从我身边经过，我都会嗅闻到她身上那股神秘的气息……
t_en: Whenever a fashionable amateur with potential to be an actress or singer passes by me, I can catch that mysterious aura on her...

t_ch: …什么气息？
t_en: ...what's that aura?

t_ch: 要红的气息、万众瞩目的气息、大把信用点愿宝的气息！
t_en: The aura of going viral, the aura of being in the spotlight, the aura of Wishbaes throwing credits around!

t_ch: 实话告诉你吧，看到你的那个瞬间，我连你以后的巡演主题名都想好了。
t_en: To be honest, the moment I saw you, I'd already thought up the name for your future tour.

t_ch: 相信我，跟着我们事务所，只要你四肢健全，我就能给你包装成寰宇影后，只要你能开口说话，成为银河歌姬也不是梦，区区知更……
t_en: Trust me, you should join our agency. As long as you've got all your limbs, I can transform you into a Universal Movie Queen. If you can open your mouth and speak, becoming a Cosmic Serenader isn't just a dream. Miss Robin is just...

t_ch: 你是说…超越知更鸟小姐？
t_en: You mean... surpassing Miss Robin?

t_ch: 嘘——这话可不敢乱讲！总之，你潜力无限，我们事务所又本事通天，强强联手，你明白意思就好！
t_en: Shh! Don't say that out loud! Look, you've got unlimited potential, and our agency is well-versed in knowledge and information. Together we'd be unstoppable, if you catch my drift!

t_ch: 我真有这么大潜力？
t_en: Do I really have that much potential?

t_ch: 说起来你可能不信，我闻到过很多种类的时尚气息，只有你的最不一样，简直是勾魂夺魄，令人心底泛痒，还有点海洋的潮腥，和面点的香甜……
t_en: You might not believe this, but I've caught whiffs of all sorts of trendy scents, and yours is utterly unique and absolutely bewitching. It makes my heart tingle, with hints of salty ocean breeze and sweet pastry all at once...

t_ch: 你闻到的其实是我刚买的大黄鱼焙面吧！
t_en: What you're smelling must be the Yellow Fish Noodles I just bought!

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: 哈哈哈…俺好幸福，好满足嘎！
t_en: Hahaha... I'm so happy, so totally stoked, quack!

t_ch: 你看起来很开心，遇到好事了？
t_en: You look happy! Did something good happen?

t_ch: 今天来集会的路上，俺竟然在垃圾桶里捡到了半碗炒面，还浇着俺最爱的酱汁嘎。
t_en: On my way to the gathering today, I actually found half a bowl of noodles in the trashcan, and it even had my favorite sauce on it, quack.

t_ch: 好久没有吃到热乎乎的饭菜，肚子里一下子暖洋洋的。
t_en: It's been so long since I've had a hot meal, the warmth spreads through my belly like a cozy blanket.

t_ch: 俺真是太幸福了嘎！
t_en: Man, I'm so freaking happy, quack!

t_ch: 你真的幸福吗？
t_en: Are you truly happy?

t_ch: 听起来很勉强。
t_en: That sounds a bit forced.

t_ch: 连、连你也这么说！
t_en: E—Even you say that!

t_ch: 和大家在一起，俺怎么可能不幸福？哈哈哈…哈哈…哈……
t_en: How could I not be happy when I'm with everyone? Hahaha... haha... ha...

t_ch: 呜…呜呜呜…呜哇哇哇哇！你说得对，俺根本就没有吃饱嘎！
t_en: Waaah... Waah... WAAAAAAH! You're right. I'm still hungry, quack!

t_ch: 你的口癖…好特别。
t_en: Your quirk... That's so special.

t_ch: 噗噜有这种口癖吗？
t_en: Do ploos really talk like that?

t_ch: 从我们那个年代起，噗噜就会「嘎、嘎」说话嘎。
t_en: Since our age, ploos have been speaking with a "quack," quack.

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你，你可别向异、异常防御部的人告密嘎。俺本来住在乡下的小溪里，饿了就去农田抓虫子吃，日子过得可自由自在了。
t_en: Let me tell you a secret, but don't snitch to the folks at the Department of Aberration Defense. I used to live in a stream out in the countryside, catching frogs from the fields whenever I got hungry. Oh, those were the days of pure freedom, quack.

t_ch: 有天我玩累了回窝里打盹，没成想一睡就睡了几百年。等被公司的挖机挖出来，才发现一切都大变样了。
t_en: One day, I got tired from playing and went back to my nest to take a nap. I didn't expect to fall asleep for hundreds of years. When the IPC's excavation machine dug it up, it was only then that we realized everything had changed.

t_ch: 农田变成了高楼，小溪变成了街道，家也找不着了嘎。
t_en: Farmlands turned into skyscrapers, streams became streets, and I can't even find my way home anymore, quack.

t_ch: 俺没有被登记在册，其他噗噜不搭理俺，也没有虫子吃，俺完全摸不清头脑，只好捡垃圾为生了。
t_en: I wasn't registered, the other ploos ignore me, and there are no bugs for me to eat. I couldn't figure it out at all, so I had to pick up trash to make a living.

t_ch: 我听到他们说，只要加入幸福微笑研究会，阿哈就会亲自让大家得到幸福，俺就来了这里。可是，虽然俺已经很努力微笑了，还是肚子好饿，不能像大家那样幸福……
t_en: I heard them say that if I joined the Research Society of Happy Smiles, Aha would personally make everyone happy, so I came here. But even though I've been trying my hardest to smile, my stomach is still so hungry, and I can't be happy like everyone else...

t_ch: 难道是没有做幸福手术的缘故嘎？
t_en: Could it be 'cause I haven't had the Happiness Surgery yet, quack?

t_ch: 这是可以做手术解决的吗？
t_en: Can this be fixed with surgery?

t_ch: 「幸福手术」是什么？
t_en: What's a "Happiness Surgery"?

t_ch: 他们和俺说，不幸福是一种疾病，是需要医治的。只要把脑袋里的不快乐切断，阿哈就会在脑子里亲自给你讲笑话，永远生活在幸福里了！
t_en: They told me that unhappiness is a disease that needs to be cured. Just cut off the unhappiness in your brain, and Aha will personally tell you jokes in your head, and you'll live in happiness forever!

t_ch: 可是俺每次想做幸福手术，大家都推三阻四，也不知道为什么。
t_en: But every time I wanna get that Happiness Surgery, everyone keeps giving me the runaround. No clue why.

t_ch: 唉，不知道什么时候才能做上幸福手术。这是我唯一的盼头了嘎！
t_en: *sigh* I wonder when I'll finally get that Happiness Surgery. That's literally the only thing I'm looking forward to, quack!

t_ch: 走了，你慢慢幸福吧。
t_en: I'm out. Have fun being happy without me.

t_ch: 你也要幸福嘎！
t_en: Be happy too, quack!

t_ch: 喂！你这家伙是同行吧？你哪个经纪公司的？这条街应该是我们「超星娱乐」的地盘吧？希望其他友商能稍微懂点规矩。
t_en: Hey! You're in the same business, aren't you? Which agency are you working for? This street should be Superstar Entertainment's turf, right? You should know a thing or two about respecting territories.

t_ch: 吃枪药了？这么凶干什么？
t_en: What's got into you? Why so hostile?

t_ch: 啊…你是「球棒浣熊」啊！对不起，这几天一直在被人抢地盘，有些应激了。看你穿得那么帅气，我还以为又是同行呢……
t_en: Ah... you're "Baseball Raccoon"! Sorry, I've been getting my territory snatched up these past few days, so I'm a bit on edge. You look so stylish, I thought you were another rival...

t_ch: 唉，看你一头雾水的样子，我还是给你解释一下吧。你应该也知道，幻造种主要靠获取愿力生活。而获取愿力最简单的方式，恐怕就是做主播了。
t_en: *sigh* You look confused. Let me explain it to you. As you probably know, the imagenae mainly survive on Wishpower. And the easiest way to get Wishpower? Well, that's probably becoming a streamer.

t_ch: 而我，就在众多赛道中，选择了「美食探店主播」。这主要是因为我有卓尔不凡的美食品味——我从十岁开始就只吃精品美食，绝不碰那些随处可见的凡庸食物！
t_en: And among all the types, I chose to be a "Food Review Streamer". This is mainly because I have an extraordinary taste in food. I've been eating only gourmet cuisine since I was ten, and I absolutely refuse to touch those mundane dishes you find everywhere!

t_ch: 可是，探店主播这条赛道也很拥挤，而且主播们各个身怀绝技。
t_en: But there were too many food review streamers, and they got their own special tricks up their sleeve.

t_ch: 有些人特别有钱，顿顿吃高级料理；有些人喜欢旅游，今天吃埃伊加汗国的汗王烤肉，明天吃克里姆特立宪国的街头卷饼；还有些料理大师，对每家店的厨艺都分析得头头是道。
t_en: Some are loaded with cash, dining on fancy cuisine every meal; some are travel foodies, feasting on Khan's BBQ in the Aigaian Khanate today and street wraps in the Klimt Republic tomorrow. Then there are the culinary connoisseurs who can break down every restaurant's cooking techniques.

t_ch: 而我…只是个很擅长吃饭的穷鬼，身为幻造种也没办法到处旅游，而且自己也不会做饭——但谁规定不会做饭就不能当美食主播？评价歼星舰还要自己会肉身跃迁？
t_en: And me... I'm just a broke foodie who happens to be good at eating. I'm an imagenae, so I cannot travel everywhere. I can't even cook, but who says you need to know how to cook to be a food streamer? Do you need to know how to warp jump yourself to share your views on a starnought?

t_ch: 所以最后，我就选择了这条道路——「小众美食探店主播」！
t_en: So in the end, I chose this path — becoming a "Niche Food Review Streamer"!

t_ch: 我到处在犄角旮旯寻找那些不为人知的「宝藏小店」，将它们「发掘」出来，使它们成为广为人知的名店，享受它们应有的客流量。
t_en: I search every nook and cranny for those hidden gem restaurants, bringing them into the spotlight so they can become well-known establishments and get the customer traffic they deserve.

t_ch: 但这就带来了另一个问题：所谓「小众美食店」，对「小众美食探店主播」而言是一种「消耗品」。任何店家一旦被我们这些顶流探店主播介绍，就会很快成为超级网红店。
t_en: But that creates another issue. For a streamer like me who specializes in hidden foodie gems, these little-known spots are essentially a "consumable resource." The moment a top streamer like me reviews a place, it blows up and becomes the next big internet sensation.

t_ch: 而一旦成为超级网红店，我的优势就不存在了。刚才说的那些专业人士，就会像闻到厨余垃圾的大鸦一样聚集过来，不给我留任何立锥之地。
t_en: And once a place goes viral, my edge is gone. The so-called "professionals" I mentioned will flock to it like vultures to a carcass, elbowing me out completely.

t_ch: 为了维持生存，我每周都要探访几家不为人知的小众美食店。但二相乐园可不会每周都凭空多开好几家小众美食店啊！
t_en: I have to hunt down a few undiscovered eateries every week so I can make a living, but it's not like Planarcadia is going to magically spawn a fresh batch of obscure shops for me every seven days!

t_ch: 之前读到过，在如今的「能源后匮乏时代」到来之前，古人们管那些几亿年才能重新出现的化石能源，叫做「不可再生资源」。「小众美食店」对我来说，就是「不可再生资源」。
t_en: I read that before we entered this "post-energy scarcity era," the ancients called fossil fuels "non-renewable resources." Because they take hundreds of millions of years to come back. That's exactly what these little-known restaurants are to me: a non-renewable resource.

t_ch: 况且，这条主播赛道虽然并不拥挤，但也有为数不少的同行…所以刚才当我以为你是同行时，才会有那么强的敌意，真不好意思啊……
t_en: On top of that, while my particular corner of the streaming world isn't exactly crowded, there's still a fair number of competition out there. That's why I was so aggressive when I mistook you for one of them. Sorry about that...

t_ch: 唉，仔细想想也挺没意思的，为了混口饭吃，我们这些主播之间像是领地意识过强的野狗一样，见面5秒就开始战斗。上个月还出现流血事件了，搞得像混混一样…何必呢？
t_en: *sigh* But it all feels so pointless, you know? We streamers are at each other's throats like territorial strays just to scrape by, ready to brawl five seconds after meeting. It even got violent last month and someone actually got hurt. It's like we're a bunch of thugs... So what's the point of it all?

t_ch: 下次有机会再聊吧，「球棒浣熊」，我要去探店了。小众美食探店是个争分夺秒的营生，万一被人抢先，今天就全白忙了。
t_en: Let's chat later, "Baseball Raccoon." I gotta get back to my scouting. This niche eatery business is a race against time. If someone else gets the scoop before me, today's a total bust.

t_ch: 离我远点，我害怕疯狗。
t_en: Get away from me. I'm scared of mad dogs.

t_ch: 哼，害怕就对了！不要跑到其他主播的地盘上来探店啊！
t_en: Hmph, you'd better be scared! Don't even think about scouting for spots on another streamer's turf!

t_ch: 啊…每次听到大家分享的幸福瞬间，我都止不住眼眶里的热泪。
t_en: Oh... I just can't hold back the tears whenever I hear people sharing their moments of happiness.

t_ch: 可能是我太感性了吧？
t_en: Maybe I'm just a little too sentimental?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，老婆对我说：「爱钓鱼就去钓一辈子吧！」
t_en: Hahaha! My wife told me, "If you love fishing so much, just go fishing for your entire life!"

t_ch: 有这么善解人意的家人，我真的太幸福了！哈哈哈！
t_en: Having such an understanding wife... I'm the happiest man alive! Hahaha!

t_ch: 你好，有兴趣了解一下「幸福微笑研究会」吗？
t_en: Hi there! Would you be interested in learning about our Research Society of Happy Smiles?

t_ch: 和我们一起向阿哈祈祷吧，祈祷永恒的欢愉降临这片被污染的净土！
t_en: Come pray to Aha with us! Pray that eternal elation descends upon this sullied sanctuary!

t_ch: 岂有此理！鼠目寸光！懂不懂什么叫真正的「灵魂摇滚」——区区一百信用点…可恶，瞧不起谁呢？！
t_en: One hundred credits!? That's it!? Uncultured swine! This is "Soul Rock," the music of the soul! You have no idea who you're insulting!

t_ch: 好啦好啦，要我说啊，就你那「生锈的破锣在刮锅底」似的嗓音…人家没向治安官举报你扰民，就已经非常善良啦。
t_en: Settle down, rockstar. Let's be real, your voice sounds like a blender full of rocks. It's a miracle you haven't been slapped with a noise complaint by the security officers.

t_ch: 更何况虽然嘴上嫌弃，你收钱的动作可是一点都不慢呢……
t_en: And besides, for all your grumbling, you sure were quick to take the money...

t_ch: 你、你给我闭嘴——
t_en: That was... Oh, shut up!

t_ch: 咦…你们长得好像啊？
t_en: Huh... you two look so alike.

t_ch: 唉…确实，经常有人这么说。
t_en: *sigh* We do get that a lot, yes.

t_ch: 你搁这儿装什么呢？还「经常有人这么说」…这位朋友，请仔细看看，我俩长得可不是「好像」，而是「一模一样」才对。
t_en: What are you playing at? "We do get that a lot"? My friend, take a closer look. We don't just "look alike," we're "spitting images" of each other.

t_ch: 咳…总、总之，你有什么事吗？如你所见，本大爷是名「通灵者」，能够凭借音乐沟通鬼神——看，这家伙就是我召唤的专属幽灵……
t_en: *cough* An-Anyway, do you need something? As you can see, I am a spirit medium who can communicate with ghosts and gods through music. See, this fellow here is the exclusive ghost I've summoned...

t_ch: 才怪嘞，我就是他哦。确切来说，是他「想当歌手」的沉重执念…啊，就是那种沉甸甸、黑漆漆的情感，像沥青一样，黏腻又刺鼻……
t_en: No way. I AM him! Or more precisely, I'm his deep-seated obsession with "becoming a rockstar"... Ah, you know, that heavy, dark, twisted emotion. It's like asphalt, sticky and pungent...

t_ch: 他还没死就有幽灵了？
t_en: There's a ghost of him before he died?

t_ch: 「幽灵」可不是人死后才会出现的哦，只不过死前的执念往往最强烈罢了。像他这样又不得志又小心眼的家伙，活着也会孳生出幽灵——准确地说，我这类叫做「我执灵」。
t_en: Ghosts don't only show up after someone dies, you know. It's just that obsessions are often strongest before death. For a guy as unsuccessful and petty as him, a ghost can sprout out of him while he's still alive. To be precise, my type is called a "spirit of obsession."

t_ch: 闭嘴吧，就你话多！咳…这位朋友，别听他瞎说——对了，既然有缘遇见，你要不要考虑当我的粉丝？
t_en: Oh, just shut up! *cough* My friend, don't listen to his nonsense... By the way, since fate brought you here, have you considered becoming a fan of mine?

t_ch: 别看我现在还没什么名气，但早晚会大红大紫的。你现在入了粉籍，以后可就是元老……
t_en: I might not be famous yet, but I'm gonna make it big sooner or later. If you join the fan club now, you'll be one of the OG fans when it happens...

t_ch: 真心不建议这么做哦~毕竟这人成名的概率，比抽中隐藏款豪华扭蛋还要低呢…啊，别瞪我嘛，只是实话实说而已。
t_en: Oh, I seriously advise against that. The odds of this guy getting famous are slimmer than pulling a super rare from a deluxe capsule... Ah, don't glare at me. I'm just telling it like it is.

t_ch: 你们这是…街头表演？
t_en: Are you guys... busking?

t_ch: 刚刚好像听到什么一百块…
t_en: I thought I heard something about a hundred credits...

t_ch: 什——不、不是的，你听我说…我…本大爷只是在偶然路过时，发现这间「歌坛」被俗气所沾染，所以特意在此开坛净化一番……
t_en: Wh—Wha— No, it's not that! I uh... I was just casually passing by when I found this "karaoke bar" defiled by tackiness, so I'm performing a special purification ceremony...

t_ch: 对对对！净化对象就是老板饱受摧残的耳膜——区区一百信用点买他后半辈子听力平安，血赚！
t_en: Exactly! And he's purifying the owner's tormented eardrums! Just a hundred credits to keep his hearing for the rest of his life. It's a total steal!

t_ch: 啧，有完没完？！你今天废话怎么这么多……
t_en: Tsk, will you knock it off!? Why do you have so much to say today...

t_ch: 对啦，这位朋友，这家店会收购顾客自己唱的曲子哦~唱得越好、价格越高，如果你想赚点零用钱的话……
t_en: By the way, my friend, this shop buys songs sung by customers~ The better you sing, the higher the price. If you want to earn a little pocket money...

t_ch: 失陪一下——我现在有些话要和他好、好、聊、聊……
t_en: If you'll excuse me, I have a few things I need to have a... nice... long... talk... with him about.

t_ch: 哇啊？！怎么有人竟然会殴打自己的「我执灵」啊！太自恨了吧！
t_en: Whoa!? Who assaults their own "spirit of obsession"!? Now that's some serious self-hatred!

t_ch: （还是不要搭话了）
t_en: (Better not get involved.)

t_ch: *憋笑*…噗噗噗…噗哈哈哈哈！
t_en: *tries to hold back laughter* Pff... pfft... hahahaha!

t_ch: 你完全露馅了！
t_en: You're totally giving yourself away!

t_ch: *泪汪汪*…呜呜…呜哇哇哇哇……
t_en: *with teary eyes* Waah... Waaaaah...

t_ch: 你也没好到哪里去！
t_en: You're not doing any better!

t_ch: 你们怎么又哭又笑的？
t_en: Why are you laughing and crying at the same time?

t_ch: 呱！忘了自我介绍，我是笑点很低的喜剧明星「鸽川小子」。
t_en: Ribbit! I forgot to introduce myself. I'm the comedy star who's easily amused, Dovebrook Kid.

t_ch: 我是泪点很低的悲剧明星「海原小子」。
t_en: And I'm the tragedy star who's easily moved to tears, Seafeld Kid.

t_ch: 我们都是满愿电视台旗下的艺人，共用同一个演播室。只不过他的节目在前，我的节目在后，很多年来，我们的收视率都不相上下。
t_en: We are both artists under Many Wishes TV and share the same studio. It's just that his show is on before mine. For many years, our ratings have been neck and neck.

t_ch: 刚才你也看到了吧？那个赶车的上班族，袜子竟然穿反了！
t_en: You saw it too, right? That commuter rushing to catch his ride had his socks on the wrong way!

t_ch: 地铁别坐反就好了。真是可笑啊，噗哈哈哈哈！
t_en: I just hope he doesn't take the subway in the wrong way. How funny, hahaha!

t_ch: 为了生计疲于奔命的上班族，居然连穿好袜子的时间都没有，未免也太可悲了，呜哇哇哇哇……
t_en: A commuter so busy just trying to make a living that he doesn't even have time to put his socks on properly. Isn't that supreme tragedy? Waaaah...

t_ch: 还有刚才不小心撞到其他人的学生，一下子脸都吓绿了呢。
t_en: And that student who accidentally bumped into someone just now? She was so frightened that her face turned green.

t_ch: 她本来就是欧克吧？未免也太可笑了，噗哈哈哈哈！
t_en: But she's an orch, isn't she? Isn't that just hilarious, hahaha!

t_ch: 就因为绿色的皮肤，一辈子都会被人误解吗？未免也太可悲了，呜哇哇哇哇……
t_en: Is she going to be misunderstood her whole life just because of her green skin? Isn't that just too tragic? Waaaah...

t_ch: 会有人喜欢看这节目吗？
t_en: Does anyone actually enjoy watching this show?

t_ch: 嗨，有的观众就是要看到别人流眼泪才高兴呢！
t_en: Hey, some viewers just aren't happy unless they see someone shedding tears!

t_ch: 泪点在哪里？
t_en: What's the sad part?

t_ch: 我知道你泪点很低，但至少得先有泪点吧。
t_en: I know you're easily moved to tears, but there has to be a reason to cry in the first place.

t_ch: 噗噜总有发现小乐趣的眼睛，这就是我们总走上演艺事业的原因。
t_en: Ploos always have an eye for finding the little joys in life, which is why we often end up in show business.

t_ch: 对我们噗噜来说，光是站在台上就很好笑了。这可能就是乐子神赏饭吃吧？噗哈哈哈哈！
t_en: To us ploos, just standing on stage is hilarious enough. Maybe it's how The Laughter ensures we have a living? Hahaha!

t_ch: 因为生来是噗噜，就要接受被嘲笑的命运吗？未免也太可悲了，呜哇哇哇哇……
t_en: Do I have to accept a fate of being laughed at just because I was born a ploo? Isn't that just too tragic? Waaaah...

t_ch: 说真的，老哥，你要不去看看病吧？
t_en: Seriously, maybe you should go see a doctor.

t_ch: 离远一点。
t_en: Stay away from them.

t_ch: 这两个家伙，家里要请高人了。
t_en: Maybe they both should go see someone professional.

t_ch: 呦，年轻人，需要代购吗？我今天来得早，还有三个多小时就能排到了。
t_en: Hey there, young one. Need a proxy buyer? I got here early today, so I'll be at the front of the line in just over three hours.

t_ch: 你在排什么…
t_en: What are you in line for...?

t_ch: 你这消息不太灵通哦？《银河战力党》最近出了新卡，火爆得很，每天队伍都从早排到晚呢！
t_en: Oh, you're not in the loop, are you? Cosmicon Collective released new cards recently. They're a huge hit, so the line is from morning till night every day!

t_ch: 而且我跟你说，那些个商家可不厚道了——你辛辛苦苦大半天，好不容易排到了，还得搞什么「粉丝资格审查」，认不出图鉴上的随机角色就不让买！
t_en: And let me tell you, those vendors are so crafty. After you've toiled for half the day and finally made it to the front, you still have to go through a "fan qualification check." They won't let you buy if you can't recognize a random character from the index!

t_ch: 不过好在婆婆我早就把全图鉴都背下来了，交给我你就放一百个心吧。
t_en: But fortunately, this old lady has already memorized the entire index, so you can be a hundred percent sure you're in good hands with me.

t_ch: 需不需要帮忙代购呀？婆婆我干这行很久了，专业素养可是一般人比不了的。咱们这儿不仅保证不拆原盒，还支持当场开封验编号。
t_en: So, need a proxy buyer? I've been in the business a long time, and my professional standards are second to none. Not only do I guarantee the original box will be unopened, but I also support on-the-spot unsealing to check the serial number.

t_ch: 而且悄悄告诉你哦，就算是那些不好抢的官方限定礼盒，凭着婆婆的人脉，也都能给你运作运作。
t_en: And I'll let you in on a secret: even for those official limited edition gift boxes that are hard to grab, I can work some magic for you, thanks to my connections.

t_ch: 当然喽，购买门槛也是稍稍有一些的啦——咱们这边打底三倍起价，越是高罕，就越贵一些。
t_en: Of course, there's a small price to pay for entry. We start at three times the original price, and the rarer the item, the more expensive it gets.

t_ch: 你可别嫌婆婆贪心啊！那些个卡牌啊、扭蛋啊、谷子啊，溢价严重不是很正常的嘛。
t_en: Now don't you think I'm being greedy! It's perfectly normal for things like trading cards, capsule toys, and other merchandise to have inflated prices.

t_ch: 而且咱们向来都是买卖全凭个人意愿，完全不存在强买强卖的——要的就是双方都满意。
t_en: Besides, my business model has always been based on mutual consent. There's absolutely no forced selling involved. What's important is that both sides are satisfied.

t_ch: 行了，那我继续去排队了，之后要是有需要的话，记得随时来找婆婆哦！
t_en: Alright, I'm getting back in line. If you need anything later, you can always come find me!

t_ch: 不需要，谢谢。
t_en: No thanks.

t_ch: 没关系没关系，之后要是有需要的话，记得随时来找婆婆哦！
t_en: No worries, no worries. If you need anything later, just come find me!

s_en: Young Man Who Wants to Join the Research Society
t_ch: 您说的这些，真的安全吗？
t_en: Are you sure what you're talking about is safe?

t_ch: …你们的忧虑不无道理，朋友，但你可以瞧瞧我，再看看周围那些「幸福微笑研究会」的成员们——
t_en: Your concerns are valid, my friend, but take a look at me, and then at the members of the Research Society of Happy Smiles around us—

t_ch: （听听他们在聊什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 哈哈，我们这里每个人都接受过手术，你看我们像是脑子不正常的吗？
t_en: Haha, everyone here has had the surgery. Do we really seem abnormal to you?

t_ch: 当然，这也取决于你如何定义「正常」，如果成为负面情绪的奴隶算是「正常」，那我们的确不算是「正常」，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Of course, that depends on how you define "normal." If being a slave to negative emotions counts as "normal," then we'd definitely be anything but. Hahaha!

s_en: Young Man Who Wants to Join the Research Society
t_ch: 但是，过程会不会很痛苦啊？
t_en: But will the process be very painful?

t_ch: 只需轻轻一戳，全程不过几秒，就能从此远离压力与烦恼。无创无疤，还没有副作用和后遗症——甚至还是免费的！「痛苦」？你很快就会忘记这个词的含义。
t_en: With just a gentle poke, the whole procedure is over in seconds, and you'll be free from stress and worries forever. It's non-invasive, leaves no scars, and has no side effects or after-effects. And it's even free! "Painful"? You'll soon forget the meaning of that word.

t_ch: 所谓的「幸福手术」，本质上可是{RUBY_B#阿哈}欢愉之主{RUBY_E#}的恩典。你可以不信任我，但你应该信任星神的伟力。
t_en: The so-called "happiness surgery" is, in essence, a gift of grace from {RUBY_B#Aha}the Master of Elation{RUBY_E#}. You don't have to trust me, but you should trust in the great power of an Aeon.

t_ch: 不信的话，去问问那些接受了手术的人吧，他们时不时就能在脑中听到{RUBY_B#阿哈}祂{RUBY_E#}的教诲。
t_en: If you don't believe me, go and ask those who've received the surgery. They can hear {RUBY_B#Aha}THEIR{RUBY_E#} teachings in their minds from time to time.

t_ch: 怎么样，你还有什么顾虑吗？
t_en: So, do you have any other concerns?

s_en: Young Man Who Wants to Join the Research Society
t_ch: 我…加入这个研究会，应该要办不少手续吧？
t_en: I assume... there's a lot of paperwork to join this society?

t_ch: 手续？我们才不是那么迂腐的机构！欢迎加入「幸福微笑研究会」！总而言之，先笑起来吧！
t_en: Paperwork? We're not that kind of stuffy organization! Welcome to the Research Society of Happy Smiles! In any case, let's start by smiling!

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: 嘿，那边路过的朋友！需要我提供「垫底」服务吗？
t_en: Hey there, friend! Need my "last place" service?

t_ch: 那是什么服务？
t_en: What kind of service is that?

t_ch: 咦？你不认识我「垫底专家」娜尔娜吗？欸～有点受伤咯～不过既然如此，就来给你介绍一下，我究竟是做什么工作的吧！
t_en: Huh? You don't know me? I'm Narna, the "Flunky Expert." Oh, that hurts a little~ But since we're here, let me tell you all about the work I do!

t_ch: 朋友，你知道吗？「以某场比赛或考试为目标」，会大幅度提高一个人学习技能的效率，但大多数人却并不会「以比赛为目标进行学习」，你知道为什么吗？
t_en: Did you know that "having a specific competition or exam as a goal" drastically improves a person's efficiency in learning skills? But most people don't "learn with a competition in mind." Do you know why?

t_ch: 因为对「垫底」的恐惧！
t_en: That's because of the fear of "coming in last"!

t_ch: 无论人类还是幻造种，都有着厌恶失败的本性，尤其是担心自己成为最后一名。于是大家就会「准备好了再去参赛」，而你猜怎么样？你永远也准备不好！
t_en: Both humans and imagenae have an innate aversion to failure, especially the fear of being the last. So everyone thinks, "I'll compete once I'm ready," but guess what? You're never truly ready!

t_ch: 所以这种时候，就要靠我「垫底专家」娜尔娜登场了！
t_en: And that's when I, Narna the "Flunky Expert," get to take the stage!

t_ch: 如果你购买了我的服务，接下来不管你选择参加什么考试或比赛，我都一定会做得比你差！百分百为你垫底！
t_en: If you purchase my service, then no matter what exam or competition you enter next, I will absolutely perform worse than you! I'll guarantee you won't be in last place!

t_ch: 这样一来，你就可以放心参加任何比赛了。因为无论你有多烂，都可以无条件相信这一点——我更烂！
t_en: This way, you can enter any competition with total confidence. Because no matter how badly you do, you can believe one thing without a doubt: I'll be even worse!

t_ch: 听起来是个很愚蠢的服务，对吧？但你试试看就知道了，人心是很脆弱的，想让它好好干活，就要想办法哄它开心，这就是「情绪价值」的意义，嘿嘿。
t_en: Sounds like a pretty dumb service, right? But you'll understand once you try it. The human heart is fragile. To get it to perform well, you have to find ways to keep it happy. That's the meaning of "emotional value," hehe.

t_ch: 行了，就介绍到这里吧。如果需要我帮你垫底，就随时来找我吧～新客八折优惠哦。
t_en: Alright, that's basically the full introduction. If you ever need someone to take last place for you, just come find me~ New customers get a <unbreak>20%</unbreak> discount!

t_ch: 不需要，谢谢。
t_en: No thanks.

t_ch: 没关系。如果需要有人帮自己垫底，请记得来找我「垫底专家」娜尔娜哦！
t_en: No worries. If you ever need someone to take last place for you, remember to come to Narna the "Flunky Expert"!

t_ch: 这一话又又又又没了！欸…这要到何时才能完结啊？！
t_en: Not another cliffhanger! Ugh... when is this series ever going to end!?

t_ch: 断章的痛，我懂。
t_en: I know the pain of a cliffhanger all too well.

t_ch: 看来你也是「受害者」。不可饶恕！那些在关键地方断章的家伙们！
t_en: Looks like you're a "victim" too. They're unforgivable! All those authors who leave us hanging at the most critical moments!

t_ch: 如果说恋爱漫画还可以用「一旦双方互表心意就直接完结了」来拖延进度的话，这本漫画我完全想不到有什么好拖的呀！
t_en: In a romance comic, you can at least drag things out with the excuse that "the story ends once they fall in love," but I can't think of any reason at all for this comic to be delayed!

t_ch: 这还算幸运的。我追的那本漫画的作者，不知怎么的喜欢上了来自仙舟的「帝垣琼玉」，直接进入了「间歇性更新，长期性休刊」的状态。
t_en: But this is still one of the better ones. The author of the other comic I'm following somehow got hooked on Celestial Jade from the Xianzhou and has now entered a state of "intermittent updates and long-term hiatus."

t_ch: 本想着等这本漫画完结，就可以放心的离去了。现在看来，这作者是铁了心地不想让漫画完结，下次更新不知道要等到何年何月了！
t_en: I was hoping I could finally move on once this comic ends. Now it looks like the author is dead set on never finishing it. Who knows when the next update will be!?

t_ch: 我想起一位沉迷打牌的故人。
t_en: That reminds me of an old friend who was obsessed with playing cards.

t_ch: 那叫「帝垣琼玉」的玩意真有那么好玩吗？欸，像这种「不务正业」的漫画家还真不少。
t_en: Is that "Celestial Jade" thing really that fun to play? Ugh, there are quite a few comic artists who don't stick to their jobs like this.

t_ch: 我甚至听说，有个漫画家发现去参与星际和平公司的炒股要比画漫画更赚钱，干脆直接就撂笔不画了！你说这都是什么事儿啊。
t_en: I even heard that one of them discovered that playing the stock market with the Interastral Peace Corporation was more profitable than drawing comics, so they just dropped their pen and quit! I mean, what's up with that!?

t_ch: 作者也有自己的苦衷吧。
t_en: I'm sure they have their reasons.

t_ch: 欸，这么说也不是不能理解。只是不光是我，其他粉丝们也等了很久了。大家都希望故事能有一个美好的结局。
t_en: Well, I suppose that's understandable. But it's not just me. Other fans have been waiting for a long time too. We all hope the story can have a happy ending.

t_ch: 不过你倒是提醒我了，作者拖更这么长时间，不会就是因为他还没想好该怎么让故事结束吧？
t_en: You've reminded me, though. The author has been dragging this out for so long. Could it be because they haven't figured out how to end the story yet?

t_ch: 实在没办法，干脆发起一个网络投票，让粉丝们选出「你最想看到的结局」好了。
t_en: If there's truly no other way, they should just launch an online poll and let the fans vote for "the ending you want to see the most."

t_ch: 我还有事先走了。
t_en: I have other things to do. See you later.

t_ch: 欸…看来我要在这里徘徊很久了。
t_en: *sigh* It seems I'll be lingering here for a long while.

t_ch: 我还有事先走了。
t_en: I have other things to do. See you later.

t_ch: 欸…看来我要在这里徘徊很久了。
t_en: *sigh* It seems I'll be lingering here for a long while.

t_ch: 需要来份盒饭吗，阿哈严选，好吃不贵。每一口都是满满的营养呀！
t_en: Care for a meal box? It's Aha's Choice, delicious and affordable! Every bite is packed with nutrients!

t_ch: 阿哈严选？
t_en: Aha's Choice?

t_ch: 顾客有所不知呀？阿哈就是吃过我们家的盒饭后才激动得流下了眼泪呀。
t_en: Oh, you haven't heard? Aha was moved to tears after eating one of our meal boxes.

t_ch: 阿哈吃完后，感慨地说道：「为什么要让我吃到这么好吃的盒饭，如果以后吃不到了怎么办？」
t_en: After finishing it, Aha emotionally said, "Why did I have to eat such a delicious meal box? What will I do if I can't have it again?"

t_ch: 我们家的盒饭精选颗粒饱满的大米，上好的肉类，先进的冷藏密封技术。打开包装就能享用，口感就像刚做出来的一样。
t_en: Our meal boxes use carefully selected plump rice, the finest meats, and advanced refrigeration and sealing technology. You can enjoy it the moment you open the package, and it tastes just like it was freshly made.

t_ch: 会有人喜欢吃这东西？
t_en: People actually like eating this stuff?

t_ch: 那是当然！你是不知道现在的年轻人时间有多宝贵，平时上学工作，连做饭的时间都拿不出。
t_en: Of course! You have no idea how precious time is to the young people nowadays. They're so busy with school and work that they can't even find time to cook.

t_ch: 这就是我们盒饭存在的意义，能够让这些没时间做饭，又不愿意去饭店的年轻人们多一个吃饭的选择。
t_en: And that's where our meal boxes come in. They give these young people, who have no time to cook and don't want to eat at restaurants, another option for their meals.

t_ch: 之前有个顾客连续一个月都在我这里买盒饭，还因此得了一个「盒饭领域大神」的称号。
t_en: Like there was a customer who bought meal boxes from me every day for a month and even earned the title "Meal Box Sage" for it.

t_ch: 这是你现编的故事吧。
t_en: You just made that up, didn't you?

t_ch: 欸~顾客，所谓故事呀传说呀这种东西，其实都是作为「调味剂」而存在的。你像这盒饭，你买之前我给你讲一个附带的小故事，就会变得更加美味不是？
t_en: Well, things like stories and legends exist as a sort of "seasoning." Take this meal box, for example. If I tell you a little story to go with it before you buy, it'll taste even more delicious, right?

t_ch: 坦白了说，这也算是一种营销策略。现在二相乐园的年轻人不都是喜欢追求什么「情绪价值」嘛。
t_en: To be honest, it's a marketing strategy. Aren't all the young people in Planarcadia obsessed with "emotional value" these days?

t_ch: 一个普通的盒饭，如果能够加上一个故事，或是包装附带一个图案，他们就愿意拿两倍的钱来买。当然，我们家的盒饭绝对是物超所值，故事就当是附赠的啦。
t_en: For an ordinary meal box, if you add a story or put a design on the packaging, they're willing to pay double for it. Of course, our meal box is an absolute bargain, so the story is just a little extra.

t_ch: 没什么胃口。
t_en: I don't have much of an appetite.

t_ch: 那好。顾客想吃的话可以随时再来呀。
t_en: Very well. Feel free to come back anytime if you get hungry.

t_ch: 没什么胃口。
t_en: I don't have much of an appetite.

t_ch: 那好。顾客想吃的话可以随时再来呀。
t_en: Very well. Feel free to come back anytime if you get hungry.

t_ch: 这位朋友，请稍微离栅栏远一点，很危险。生活是很美好的，你可千万不要想不开呀！
t_en: Hey there, could you step a little further from the railing? It's quite dangerous. Life is beautiful, don't do anything rash!

t_ch: 多虑了，我没打算跳。
t_en: Don't worry, I wasn't planning to jump.

t_ch: 哦哦，那就好…但也不要离栏杆太近哦，出现事故也是不好的。
t_en: Oh good, that's a relief... But still, please don't stand too close to the railing. Accidents do happen, you know.

t_ch: 呜呜呜我要跳下去！
t_en: Waaah, I'm gonna jump!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，看您耍宝的样子就知道，您是非常热爱生活的。看来是我多虑了，抱歉。
t_en: Hahaha, just seeing you goof around like that tells me you really know how to enjoy life. Looks like I was worried for nothing, sorry about that.

t_ch: 初次见面，我叫黛黛丝。日常的工作，就是在这座桥上劝导往来行人珍惜生命，算是位志愿者吧。
t_en: Nice to meet you, I'm Dades. My daily job is to stand on this bridge and persuade passersby to cherish their lives. You could call me a volunteer.

t_ch: 现代生活充满虚无，每个人都被困在「存在危机」中，我们的组织需要更多像您这样热爱生活的志愿者，来阻止大家放弃生命。
t_en: Modern life is full of emptiness, and everyone's trapped in an "existential crisis." Our organization needs more life-loving volunteers like you to stop people from giving up on their lives.

t_ch: 又见面了。请问今天有什么事吗？
t_en: We meet again. What can I help you with today?

t_ch: 想多和你聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to talk with you a bit more.

t_ch: 当然可以。和人聊天本身也算是我工作的一部分，哈哈。
t_en: Of course! Talking with people is part of my job anyway, haha.

t_ch: 为什么会劝大家活着？
t_en: Why would you volunteer to convince people to stay alive?

t_ch: …我讲个故事吧，你当做别人的故事听就好——严格来说，确实就是别人的故事。
t_en: ...Let me tell you a story. Just think of it as someone else's story. Because, strictly speaking... it is.

t_ch: 以前有个女孩，她出生在一个没有任何出众之处的小城市。而她自己也相貌普通、成绩普通、能力普通，没有任何出众之处。
t_en: Once there was a girl who was born in an utterly unremarkable small town. She herself was just as unremarkable: an ordinary face, ordinary grades, ordinary skills. Nothing about her stood out.

t_ch: 那座小城很小，小到每个人都认识彼此。那座小城又很大，大到这个女孩怎么也走不出去。好像她注定要在这里渡过自己波澜不惊的一生。
t_en: The city was small, so small that everyone knew each other. Yet it was so large that this girl could never find her way out. It seemed she was destined to spend her unremarkable life here.

t_ch: 吃饭、睡觉、上学。今天和明天一模一样，明天和后天一模一样…想必即使从学校毕业，这样无限循环的生活也不会有丝毫改变吧？
t_en: Eat, sleep, go to school. Today is exactly the same as tomorrow, and tomorrow is exactly the same as the day after... Guess even after graduating, this endless cycle of life won't change one bit, huh?

t_ch: 于是女孩觉得，她看到了「尽头」。如果人生的「此刻」和「最后一刻」不会有任何区别，那何不让「最后一刻」提前到来呢？
t_en: And so the girl thought she had seen the end. If there would be no difference between the present and final moment of life, why not let that final moment come sooner?

t_ch: 于是她纵身一跃，希望这无尽的苦闷可以彻底结束。然而就在半空中，一个前所未有的念头涌现在她的脑海中——
t_en: So she took a leap, hoping this endless torment would finally come to an end. But in midair, an unprecedented thought surged through her mind—

t_ch: 她「想要活下去」。她前所未有地「想要活下去」。比火焰渴望木柴还「想要活下去」，比宇宙渴望群星还「想要活下去」。
t_en: She wanted to live. She wanted to live like never before. More than fire craves wood, more than the universe yearns for its stars, she wanted to live.

t_ch: 「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」「想要活下去」！
t_en: "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!" "I want to live!"

t_ch: 她应声坠地，再也不动。但她那「想要活下去」的强烈欲念，却在幻月的光辉下得以显化，此刻正站在你面前。
t_en: She hit the ground, never to move again. Yet, under the glow of Phantasmoon, her burning will to live manifested. There it stands before you now.

t_ch: 所以回到最初的问题，「为什么会劝大家活着？」因为我自己「想要活下去」。哈哈，就只是这样以己度人的理由罢了。
t_en: So, back to the original question: Why would I volunteer to convince people to stay alive? Because I myself want to stay alive. Haha, it's just me projecting my own feelings onto others, that's all.

t_ch: 你做志愿者多久了？
t_en: How long have you been volunteering?

t_ch: 我想想…好像有个<unbreak>30</unbreak>、<unbreak>40</unbreak>年了？不太记得了，反正至少<unbreak>30</unbreak>年。哎呀，不知不觉都干了这么久了。
t_en: Let me think... It's been <unbreak>30</unbreak>, maybe <unbreak>40</unbreak> years? Can't quite remember, but at least <unbreak>30</unbreak> years for sure. Wow, time really flies. I've been at it for so long without even realizing.

t_ch: 你应该知道吧？我们「幽灵」并不是「人死后的灵魂」，而是强烈执念的显化。所以基本上，我们都是「偏执狂」，会持续执着于同一件事。
t_en: You probably know this, right? We "ghosts" aren't "souls of the dead," but the manifestation of intense obsessions. So basically, we're all "obsessives," forever fixated on one specific thing.

t_ch: 说起来，这些年二相乐园变化还挺大的。以前我做的这种公益事业，基本上只能靠好心人募捐来维系，毕竟没有任何收益嘛。
t_en: Come to think of it, Planarcadia has changed quite a bit over the years. Back then, these charitable works I did could only be sustained through donations from kind-hearted people, since there wasn't any profit in it, you know.

t_ch: 而现在…我最近签了经纪公司，每周要直播两次，在「抖妖」上做公益宣讲，还挺火的。每次直播，都有很多人打赏。
t_en: And now... I recently signed with a talent agency. I need to stream twice a week and do public welfare talks on OrchTok. It's pretty hot right now. Every time I stream, lots of people send me gifts.

t_ch: 说来真是荒谬，我竟然靠劝别人活下去赚到钱了…哎呀，真是哭笑不得。
t_en: It's absurd when you think about it... I actually made money by convincing others to stay alive. Honestly, I don't know whether to laugh or cry about this.

t_ch: 我本来有点膈应这种「公益商业化」，已经不想继续直播了。但后来，我在后台收到了很多年轻人的来信，他们大都和「我」以前差不多，觉得人生无趣，对未来无所适从。
t_en: I was initially put off by this "commercialization of charity" and ready to quit streaming. But later, I received many letters from young people in my inbox. Most of them were similar to how I used to be: lost, bored with life, and struggling with an uncertain future.

t_ch: 这些年轻人感谢我，说多亏了我的劝导，才重新找到了活下去的动力。
t_en: These young people thanked me, saying it was my guidance that restored their will to live again.

t_ch: 这样看来，「公益商业化」也不完全是件坏事嘛！至少，我能尽可能让发生在「我」身上的故事，不再在二相乐园重演。
t_en: Looking at it this way, "commercializing" charity isn't entirely a bad thing after all. At least I can do my best to prevent what happened to me from repeating in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 你觉得，人生的意义是什么？
t_en: What do you think is the meaning of life?

t_ch: 我只是个劝导员，并不是个哲学家。这样宏大的问题，我实在有点答不上来。
t_en: I'm just a counselor, not a philosopher. That's a bit too big a question for me to chew on.

t_ch: 但我清楚知道一件事：「活着」是一件很苦闷的事，年龄越大越是如此。等到一定岁数，就再也体验不到「身体和精神都很舒服」的感受了。
t_en: But I know one thing for sure: Being alive is a miserable thing, and it only gets worse with age. Once you reach a certain age, that state of "being at peace within your own body and mind" will elude you forever.

t_ch: 但是，明明生活充满无趣、充满痛苦、充满遗憾、充满永远憧憬却永远也不会发生的事，但我们还是会选择「活下去」，迎接下一个烦恼的日子。
t_en: Yet, even though life is full of boredom, pain, regret, and dreams that never come true, we still choose to keep on living, welcoming each new day with its troubles.

t_ch: 我猜想恐怕是因为，总体而言，生活是很美好的。
t_en: I guess it's probably because, all things considered, life is pretty wonderful.

t_ch: 倘若并非如此，就去让它变得美好吧。体认幸与不幸，并为之做出改变，这是智慧生命的特权，不该平白浪费。
t_en: If that's not the case, then make it wonderful. To understand fortune and misfortune and to adapt accordingly is the privilege of intelligent life, one that must not be wasted.

t_ch: 哎呀…好像跑题了。不过也没关系吧，反正我也答不出你原本的问题。
t_en: Oops... seems I've gone off track. But it's fine, right? I couldn't answer your original question anyway.

t_ch: 就先聊到这吧！
t_en: Let's stop here for now.

t_ch: 拜拜，要热爱生活哦。也要记得关心身边的人，及时察觉他们释放出的「求救信号」。
t_en: Bye-bye! Remember to embrace life, okay? And don't forget to care for those around you. Stay alert for any distress signals they might be sending out.

t_ch: 其实我想知道…
t_en: Actually, I want to know...

t_ch: 拜拜，要热爱生活哦。也要记得关心身边的人，及时察觉他们释放出的「求救信号」。
t_en: Bye-bye! Remember to embrace life, okay? And don't forget to care for those around you. Stay alert for any distress signals they might be sending out.

t_en: Hmm, looks like you do know something about mech identification. How about hitting that follow button? I promise you won't regret it!

t_ch: 我看起来像是机铠吗？
t_en: Do I look like a mechatron to you?

t_ch: 哈哈，抱歉，误会了。我之前听人说「球棒浣熊有时候做事挺人机的」，还以为这个「人机」是「人型机铠」的意思呢！
t_en: Haha, I misunderstood, my apologies. I heard people saying "Baseball Raccoon can be quite robotic sometimes," and I actually thought "robotic" was referring to a human-mechatron!

t_ch: 我会为大家提供二维市最详细的共享机体一站式选用指南——挑选机体不用愁，「接头霸王」来接头！
t_en: I'll provide you with the most comprehensive one-stop guide for shared mech selection in Duomension City. "The Mech Matchmaker: Your Go-To Guide for Finding the Perfect Build!"

t_ch: 这具机体的各项测试均已完成，评价为：平平无奇！该换下一具了。
t_en: All tests on this mech unit are complete. Verdict: A Solid Meh! Time to move on to the next one.

t_ch: 哦，是你啊。关于替换机体，你是不是还有什么想了解的？那你可就找对人了——挑选机体不用愁，「接头霸王」来接头！
t_en: Oh, it's you. Got questions about switching mechs? Well, you've come to the right person! "The Mech Matchmaker: Your Go-To Guide for Finding the Perfect Build!"

t_ch: 想要交流交流。
t_en: Just want to have a chat.

t_ch: 没问题，正好我也趁此机会评测一下扬声器系统。
t_en: Sure thing. Perfect chance to test out my speaker system too.

t_ch: 怎么会想到这个赛道？
t_en: What got you into this field?

t_ch: 前阵子我一直租用的共享机体，商家经营不善跑路，押金也拒不退还了。评价为：无良奸商！
t_en: The company I was renting a shared mech from totally flopped and ran away with my deposit! Verdict: Unscrupulous Scumbag!

t_ch: 我一怒之下，在「抖妖」开帖怒喷。没成想，竟然大受欢迎！
t_en: Furious, I made a post ranting about it on OrchTok. To my surprise, it went viral!

t_ch: 义愤填膺的机铠兄弟、感同身受的幻造种、围观看热闹的人类…一瞬间浏览量飙升，成千上万回复沿着网线从二相乐园各地涌进评论区，点赞提示音响彻了整个夜晚！
t_en: Outraged mechatron brothers, sympathetic imagenae, humans just here for the drama... the views skyrocketed instantly! Thousands upon thousands of comments poured in from all corners of Planarcadia, while notification chimes rang throughout the entire night!

t_ch: 每个人都可以成名十五分钟！
t_en: Everyone can be famous for fifteen minutes!

t_ch: 真令人兴奋啊…宝贝，这就是哈托彼亚！
t_en: What a rush... darling, this is Ahatopia!

t_ch: 为什么不调成免打扰模式？
t_en: Why not switch to Do Not Disturb mode?

t_ch: 为什么要呢？我就是在享受万众瞩目的感觉啊。
t_en: Why should I? I was basking in the spotlight!

t_ch: 我因此得到的愿力已经远远超过了押金的损失，甚至比我踏踏实实工作半年积攒的愿力还要多。这种大好机会要是不抓住，我不就和那些噗噜老弟一样呆了吗？
t_en: The Wishpower I gained from this far exceeded the deposit I lost, even more than what I'd save up from half a year of honest work. If I didn't seize such a golden opportunity, wouldn't I be just as dumb as those ploo fellows?

t_ch: 呃，没有不尊重噗噜粉丝的意思。
t_en: Uh, no offense to my ploo followers, of course.

t_ch: 总之，我趁此机会起号，之后一直测评机铠机体。虽然热度不及起初，但收入也算很可观了。
t_en: Anyway, I took that chance to start my channel and kept reviewing mechatron units since then. Though the hype isn't what it used to be, the income's still pretty decent.

t_ch: 如何辨别机体好坏呢？
t_en: How can you tell if a mech is good or bad?

t_ch: {F#姐们}{M#哥们}，你真是问到点子上了。评价为：机铠行家！
t_en: {F#Sis}{M#Bro}, you hit the nail on the head. Verdict: Mechatron Expert!

t_ch: {F#姐们}{M#哥们}也算和大机器人交过手。
t_en: Well, I've had my share of fights with some big robot.

t_ch: 多亏了杨叔的耳濡目染。
t_en: Mr. Yang's influence really rubbed off on me.

t_ch: 鉴别机体好坏，除了要关注外观、功能这些老生常谈，最重要的就是分辨「生产批次」。
t_en: See, when evaluating a mech's quality, besides the usual stuff like appearance and functionality, the most crucial thing is identifying its "production batch."

t_ch: 早期批次，模具表面细腻、涂装均匀，版件间拼接很紧密。公司为了压缩成本，后期批次用的都是廉价材料，生产工艺也越来越敷衍了。
t_en: Early batches had refined molds, clean paint, and tight panel fitting. To cut costs, the IPC later used cheap materials and got increasingly sloppy with their production quality.

t_ch: 呵呵…能够准确地通过生产编号辨认批次，正是我在众多拙劣的跟风模仿者中永立潮头的秘诀。评价为：独家秘方！
t_en: Heh-heh... Being able to accurately identify batches through production numbers is exactly what keeps me riding high while all the lousy copycats eat my dust. Verdict: Exclusive Formula!

t_ch: 为什么要用共享机体？
t_en: Why use shared mechs anyway?

t_ch: 相比驱动机体一步步走到目的地，「只送脑袋」耗费的愿力要少得多。于是便有商家盯上了这个商机，做起了租借「共享机体」的生意。
t_en: Compared to piloting a mech all the way to your destination, "head-only delivery" consumes way less Wishpower. Naturally, business sharks spotted this opportunity and started offering "shared mech" rental services.

t_ch: 就拿搬货来说吧。搬完货后，不必拖着沉重的身躯，只需要让头飞回来，就有一具新身体等着自己，这种畅快感，就像从清晨梦乡里诞生的第一缕愿力一样甜蜜！
t_en: Take cargo hauling, for example. Once the job's done, instead of dragging your heavy metallic torso home, you just need to fly your head back. And et voilà! A fresh body's waiting! That feeling of freedom is as sweet as the first wisp of Wishpower born from your morning dreams!

t_ch: 而我们需要付出的，只是一些押金和租金。当然啦，也拜他们所赐，我当上了测评博主，不用再干体力活了。
t_en: And all we need to pay is just some deposit and rent. Thanks to them, I became a review influencer and don't have to do manual labor anymore.

t_ch: 不过说实话，每天换一具身体，走两步，写测评，还没来得及适应，就再换一具身体…这种生活，我也已经有点厌倦了。
t_en: To be honest, I'm getting tired of this life: Switching bodies every day, taking a few steps, writing reviews, and having to switch again before I even get used to the current one...

t_ch: 确实也有许多机铠，愿意把愿力挥霍在珠星大厦的零部件货架上，只为了打造一台独属于自己的机体，像人类那样去爱护。
t_en: There are indeed many mechatrons who are willing to splurge their Wishpower on shelves of Pearluxe Tower's parts, just to build a mech they can call their own and cherish it like humans do.

t_ch: 但如果像他们一样…我不就失去这份「机体测评博主」的工作了吗？
t_en: But if I end up like them... wouldn't I lose my job as a mechatron review influencer?

t_ch: 是时候转型生活区博主了。
t_en: Time to rebrand yourself as a lifestyle influencer.

t_ch: 过普通生活也没什么不好。
t_en: There's nothing wrong with living a normal life.

t_ch: 唉…那种被一万道目光注视的感觉，体验过一次，就再也忘不了了。
t_en: *sigh*... Once you've experienced that feeling of being watched by ten thousand pairs of eyes, you can never forget it.

t_ch: 就先聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 没问题，{F#姐们}{M#哥们}。有空的话，记得点赞关注收藏我的测评——挑选机体不用愁，「接头霸王」来接头！
t_en: No problem, {F#miss}{M#mister}! When you get the chance, don't forget to like, follow, and favorite my review: "The Mech Matchmaker: Your Go-To Guide for Finding the Perfect Build!"

t_ch: 不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 没问题，{F#姐们}{M#哥们}。有空的话，记得点赞关注收藏我的测评——挑选机体不用愁，「接头霸王」来接头！
t_en: No problem, {F#miss}{M#mister}! When you get the chance, don't forget to like, follow, and favorite my review: "The Mech Matchmaker: Your Go-To Guide for Finding the Perfect Build!"

t_ch: 这股强大的气息…不同于那些被囚禁在名为校服的「囚牢」中的凡人，你究竟是谁？
t_en: This powerful aura... Unlike those mortals imprisoned in the "prison" called school uniforms, who exactly are you?

t_ch: 你又是谁？报上名来！
t_en: Right back at you! Identify yourself!

t_ch: 我是绘世学院的一名普通学生，也是拯救世界之人。其真名为…月下勇者•小红。
t_en: I am but an average student at Graphia Academy, and also the one who will save the world. My true name is... "Valor Moon: Reddy."

t_ch: 所以你叫小红是吧…
t_en: So you're Reddy, huh...

t_ch: 若你执意要和那群凡人一样使用这个称呼，我不介意。
t_en: If you insist on using that title like those mortals do, I don't mind.

t_ch: 我懂了，是中二病！
t_en: I get it, this is delusions of grandeur!

t_ch: 我知晓你的不屑，但我早已习以为常。
t_en: I know your contempt for me, but I've long gotten used to it.

t_ch: 作为拯救世界之人，我无心理会他人的闲言蜚语，因为我很忙。
t_en: As someone destined to save the world, I'm too busy to care about idle gossip.

t_ch: 每天的「日月交汇时分」，我就需要背负着「智慧的诅咒」，斩除「邪祟势力」的侵蚀，踏上「孤独远征」。
t_en: Every day at the "Hour of Sun and Moon's Convergence," I must bear the "Curse of Wisdom" and embark on a "Solitary Expedition" to cleanse the corruption of the "Fiendling Forces."

t_ch: 最后，我将抵达「世界赐福之地」，并「领受爱的恩赐」。
t_en: Finally, I shall reach the "Land of World's Benediction" and "Receive the Gift of Love."

t_ch: 听不懂吗？好吧，我解释一下。
t_en: Don't you get it? Alright, let me explain.

t_ch: 意思是：每天{RUBY_B#日月交汇时分}放学后{RUBY_E#}，我会背着{RUBY_B#智慧的诅咒}装满作业的书包{RUBY_E#}，穿过{RUBY_B#邪祟势力}各种社团{RUBY_E#}的地盘。
t_en: It means: Every day {RUBY_B#Hour of Sun and Moon's Convergence}after school{RUBY_E#}, I carry my {RUBY_B#Curse of Wisdom}backpack full of homework{RUBY_E#} through the territories of {RUBY_B#Fiendling Forces}various clubs{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 然后{RUBY_B#踏上孤独远征}一个人走回家{RUBY_E#}，把自己锁在{RUBY_B#世界赐福之地}卧室{RUBY_E#}里。
t_en: Then {RUBY_B#Solitary Expedition}walking home alone{RUBY_E#}, locking myself in my {RUBY_B#Land of World's Benediction}bedroom{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 所以你还挺清醒的？
t_en: So you're still pretty lucid, huh?

t_ch: 不，我始终认为我在拯救世界——比如，在「不加入社团就会不合群」的「罪恶现实」中，仍然坚守身为「宅家女郎」的信仰。
t_en: No, I've always believed I was saving the world. Like, staying true to my stay-at-home gal beliefs in this "evil reality" where not joining clubs makes you an outcast.

t_ch: 为了这个伟大目标，我需要回家{RUBY_B#领受爱的恩赐}看动漫{RUBY_E#}了——美好的幻想世界啊，请赋予我对抗黑暗的力量吧！
t_en: For this noble cause, I need to go home and {RUBY_B#Receive the Gift of Love}watch cartoons{RUBY_E#}. Oh, this beautiful world of fantasy, grant me the power to fight against darkness!

t_ch: 先不和你聊了。
t_en: I'll talk to you later.

t_ch: 果然，世上的其他人都不能理解我啊……
t_en: Just as I thought, no one else in this world understands me...

t_ch: 嘿！你！没错，就是你。和我签订{RUBY_B#合同}契约{RUBY_E#}，成为魔法少女吧！
t_en: Hey! You! Yes, you! Make a {RUBY_B#pact}contract{RUBY_E#} with me and become a magical girl!

t_ch: 魔法少女是什么？
t_en: What's a magical girl?

t_ch: 相信爱与梦想的年轻人，在「机缘巧合」下获得魔法知识，与邪恶势力勇敢战斗，完成大人们都难以完成的壮举——
t_en: A young person who believes in love and dreams, who stumbles upon magical knowledge through sheer luck, and who bravely battles evil forces in combat, accomplishing heroic feats that even adults couldn't dream of—

t_ch: 她们会被当成和幻造类似的特别存在，被民众们亲切地称呼为「魔法少女」。
t_en: They're treated as special entities similar to imagenae, affectionately dubbed "magical girls" by the public.

t_ch: 而我们{RUBY_B#MGT}魔法少女急袭剧团{RUBY_E#}，便是一家为魔法少女们提供支援，进行整编，与治安官合作调度任务的服务性企业。
t_en: And our {RUBY_B#MGT}Magical Girl Troupe{RUBY_E#} is a service company that provides support to magical girls, organizes them into units, and coordinates missions in cooperation with security officers.

t_ch: 安保公司？
t_en: A security company?

t_ch: …对，可以这么理解。但我们的业务受官方承认和资助，且雇员多半是年轻人，所以无论是劳务费、补贴还是工作环境方面，都比一般安保公司更有竞争力。
t_en: ...Yeah, you could say that. But our operation's officially recognized and funded, and most of our staff are young, so whether it's pay, benefits, or work environment, we're way more competitive than your average security company.

t_ch: 有这么多魔法少女吗？
t_en: There are that many magical girls?

t_ch: 这就是我们的第二个主要职能了——为憧憬魔法的年轻人提供专业的契约顾问服务，帮助新的魔法少女顺利诞生。
t_en: And that brings us to our second major function: providing professional contract consulting services for young people longing for magic. We help new magical girls blossom.

t_ch: 至于具体活动，日常训练等等细则，可以在闲暇时浏览我们的直播间，从而了解更多。
t_en: As for the specifics: activities, daily training routines, and all that jazz. Feel free to check out our live stream whenever you've got time to learn more.

t_ch: 我也能成为{F#魔法少女}{M#魔法少男}吗？
t_en: Can I become a {F#magical girl}{M#magical boy} too?

t_ch: 当然，{F#我们随时欢迎新生加入我们！}{M#不过最近「魔法少男」的岗位有些饱和，你可以过段时间再来打听一下。}
t_en: Of course, {F#we always welcome Rebirths to join us!}{M#although the 'Magical Boy' positions are a bit saturated lately, you might wanna check back in a while.}

t_ch: 另外，考虑到魔法装备的损耗率，我们暂时不接受兼职申请。每月的总出勤量需要达到二百系统时才行。
t_en: Also, considering the wear and tear on magical equipment, we're not taking part-time applications at the moment. You'll need to clock at least two hundred system hours per month.

t_ch: 就聊到这吧。
t_en: Let's leave it at that.

t_ch: 好的，如果有兴趣，请随时联系我！
t_en: Sure thing! If you're interested, hit me up anytime!

t_ch: 锵锵！恭喜同学！你已成为回家社第<unbreak>100</unbreak>名社员！欢迎欢迎，热烈欢迎！
t_en: Ta-da! Congratulations, my friend! You've just become the <unbreak>100</unbreak>th member of the Go-Home Club! Welcome, welcome, a warm welcome to you!

t_ch: 我不是绘世学院的学生。
t_en: I'm not a student of Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 真的吗？我看你还挺像绘世学院的学生的。
t_en: Really? You look like one to me though.

t_ch: 欸？同学难道不是来加入我们回家社的吗？我看你好像不是很了解的样子。
t_en: Huh? Aren't you here to join our Go-Home Club? You seem a bit clueless about it.

t_ch: 只是个路过的球棒侠而已。
t_en: Just your friendly neighborhood Baseballer.

t_ch: 球棒侠？哦哦，同学是棒球社的社员吗？
t_en: Baseballer? Oh, are you from the Baseball Club?

t_ch: 看来同学的确没有了解过呢。简单介绍一下，回家社就是放学后回家的社团。不过我们可不是真的回家哦，而是社员一起来到活动室进行社团活动。
t_en: Guess you really haven't heard of us. Lemme explain: the Go-Home Club is a club for students who go home after school. But! We don't actually go home. Instead, all club members gather in the clubroom for activities.

t_ch: 有的时候会一起看电影，有的时候也会一起打牌…啊对了！我们的新社员前几天拿来了自己的游戏机，现在还可以一起打游戏了。
t_en: Sometimes we watch movies together, sometimes we play cards... Oh right! The other day our new members brought their gaming consoles, so now we can play video games together too.

t_ch: 我们回家社可是整个绘世学院最最火爆的社团哦！所以我看到你走上前来，就以为你是要加入我们社团的新同学。
t_en: Our Go-Home Club is the absolute hottest club in all of Graphia Academy! That's why when I saw you coming over, I thought you were a new student wanting to join us.

t_ch: 有多火爆？
t_en: How hot are we talking?

t_ch: 同学刚才也听到了吧？你是回家社的第<unbreak>100</unbreak>名社员欸！<unbreak>100</unbreak>名！有些社团的历史成员加起来都没这么多。
t_en: You heard me just now, right? You're the <unbreak>100</unbreak>th member of the Go-Home Club! <unbreak>100</unbreak>th! Some clubs don't even have that many members in their entire history.

t_ch: 直接回家玩不好吗？
t_en: Wouldn't it be easier to just go home and play?

t_ch: 哼哼。这就是同学不懂了。回家玩和在学校玩能一样吗？我们可是光明正大地打着社团活动的名义在玩游戏，绝不是一回事好嘛。
t_en: Hehe. That's where you're wrong, my friend. What we do here is a world apart from mere gaming at home: it's a fully authorized club activity, which makes it entirely aboveboard!

t_ch: 可能我有点激动了，要是吓到你了不好意思。我也并不是要你现在就做出决定，毕竟加入社团这事还是要好好考虑一下的。
t_en: Sorry if I got a bit carried away and freaked you out. I'm not asking you to make up your mind right now. Joining a club is indeed something you should think through.

t_ch: 如果感兴趣，想要加入的话可以随时来找我哦。
t_en: If you're interested, you can always come find me later.

t_ch: 绘世学院最火爆的社团正在迎新中！路过的各位同学不来了解一下嘛？
t_en: Graphia Academy's hottest club is recruiting! Hey guys, why not check us out?

t_ch: 我想要了解一下社团情况。
t_en: I'd like to learn more about the club.

t_ch: 当然可以！社团成员、社团活动、新人福利…身为社长的我知无不言，言无不尽呀。
t_en: Of course! As the club president, I'll tell you everything there is to know about our members, activities, perks for newcomers... you name it!

t_ch: 回家社究竟是怎么来的？
t_en: How did the Go-Home Club come to be?

t_ch: 大部分同学也问过这个问题，前辈们说回家社最初根本就没有多少社员，是一届一届社长们的努力，才把回家社建设成了今天这个样子。
t_en: Ah, that's a question lots of students ask. According to our seniors, the club didn't have many members in the beginning. It took generations of club presidents to build it up to what it is today.

t_ch: 在最一开始，老师们一致认为「回家社」不允许审批，理由是社团活动和目的模糊不清。老师们说的也没错就是啦。
t_en: At first, the teachers unanimously rejected the "Go-Home Club" application, saying the club's activities and purpose were too vague. Well, can't really blame them for that one.

t_ch: 学校里的社团种类十分丰富，田径社、漫画社、游戏社、甚至还有电影研究会…每个社团都有明确的社团活动和目的。
t_en: The campus has a rich variety of clubs: Track and Field, comic club, gaming club, and even a film research fan club... all with clear purposes and activities.

t_ch: 而回家社并非上面的任何一种。上一届的学长说，它真正的主旨是「做自己想做的事情」。那些难以归类为某一单一形式的活动，就可以在回家社完成。
t_en: But the Go-Home Club didn't fit any of those. As last year's senior said, its true purpose is "doing whatever you want to do." Any activity that can't be neatly categorized into a single form can be done here in the Go-Home Club.

t_ch: 这就是「回家」的最初含义，就是因为找不到一个能够完美满足自己的社团才选择的回家。所以…去加入社团吧，去加入不被定义的社团！——学长当时如是说道。
t_en: That's the original meaning of "Go-Home." It started with students who couldn't find a club that truly fit them, so they decided to go home. Then... go join a club, one that defies all definitions! That's how our seniors put it.

t_ch: 在老前辈们的据理力争和其他同学的提议之下，最终还是成功设立了回家社并且获得了活动室。那一天是回家社最最重要，也是最值得纪念的一天。
t_en: After plenty of heated debates and a few clever proposals from other students, our seniors finally succeeded in establishing the Go-Home Club and securing a clubroom. That day became the most important and memorable day in the Go-Home Club's history.

t_ch: 这就是「回家社成立纪念日」，那一天大家会在活动室举办派对，纪念那一段回家社建立的历史。嘿嘿，这也算是独属于我们的文化啦。
t_en: This is Go-Home Club Foundation Day. On that day, everyone gathers in the clubroom for a party to commemorate our founding. Hehe, it's kinda our own little tradition.

t_ch: 入部有什么考核需求吗？
t_en: Are there any requirements to join?

t_ch: 的确是有的。既然我们叫做「回家社」，那么最基本的要求就是一定要能「宅得住」。
t_en: That's right. Since we're called the "Go-Home Club," the most basic requirement is that you must be capable of "staying home."

t_ch: 这还不简单？
t_en: Isn't that easy?

t_ch: 可不要小瞧了这个能力，很多同学都做不到呢。有些同学就是「宅不住」，一定要出门到处走走才行。
t_en: Oh, don't underestimate that ability! A lot of students can't even manage it. Some just can't stay home, they absolutely have to go out and about all the time.

t_ch: 有点难度。
t_en: Sounds a little bit difficult.

t_ch: 的确有同学也是这样想的。看来你们同属于「宅不住」的类型。我也能够理解，有的时候就是想出门到处走走才行。
t_en: Yeah, quite a few students said the same thing. Looks like you're also part of the "can't-stay-home" type. I get it though, sometimes you just have to get out and stretch your legs.

t_ch: 回家社之前举办过一届「回家社宅在家里最长时间比赛」。获胜的是一位噗噜社员，他在家里整整宅了六个月的时间没有出门！
t_en: We actually held a "Longest Stay-at-Home Contest" once. The winner was a ploo member who stayed home for six whole months without stepping outside!

t_ch: 因为实在是太久了，以至于大家都快要忘记他的存在了。比赛结束的事情也忘记通知他了。你知道吧，如果幻造种长时间没人记得，就会逐渐消失。
t_en: He stayed in for so long that everyone kind of forgot he existed. We even forgot to tell him the contest was over. You know how it goes: if no one remembers an imagenae for too long, they start to fade away.

t_ch: 还好期末考试时大家拼尽全力终于想起了到底是谁缺席了，不然就出大事了。所以从那以后规定，如果社员一定想要长时间宅在家里的话，就来经常有人的活动室。
t_en: Thankfully, during final exams, everyone wracked their brains and finally remembered who was missing. Otherwise, things could've gone bad. So after that, we made it a rule: if members really want to spend a long time in one place, they should do it in the clubroom where people are around.

t_ch: 为什么回家社这么火爆？
t_en: Why is the Go-Home Club so popular?

t_ch: 欸！这你可就问到点子上了。其实之前很多人都提出过疑问：回家社的这些活动都能在家完成，那我为什么不直接回家呢？
t_en: Hey! You've asked just the right question. Actually, a lot of people have raised this question before: if all these Go-Home Club activities can be done at home, why don't I just go straight home?

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你，我这个当社长的其实一开始也问过这个问题。但是只有当你真正加入了社团，参与了社团活动后，才知道回家社是有多么的不一样！
t_en: Between you and me, I actually wondered that myself when I first joined club. But it's only after you actually join and take part in club activities that you'll discover just how unique the Go-Home Club really is!

t_ch: 其实加入回家社的同学们都是害怕孤独的孩子们。大家希望能够彼此交流自己感兴趣的东西，也希望能够分享自己的快乐。这不是一个人在家里就可以做到的。
t_en: You see, most of our club members are kids who don't like being alone. They want to share what they love, talk about their hobbies, and laugh together. That's not something you can do by yourself at home.

t_ch: 就像我看网络上说的：游戏的最高配置其实是可以一起玩的朋友。我表示十分赞同！如果没有人陪我，现在我都不自己一个人玩游戏了。
t_en: You know what they say online: the ultimate gaming setup is friends to play with. I couldn't agree more! These days, I don't even bother playing games alone if there's no one to join me.

t_ch: 大家都还是挺容易害羞的，不愿意公开的谈论自己的爱好。所以回家社某种意义上也算是一个交流爱好的俱乐部啦。我也是通过这里，才认识了不少志同道合的人呢。
t_en: People tend to be quite shy and reluctant to openly discuss their hobbies. So in a way, the Go-Home Club is also a little community for people with shared passions. Thanks to this place, I've actually met quite a few like-minded people.

t_ch: 没什么想问的。
t_en: That's all I wanted to know.

t_ch: 那好，等你有什么问题了可以随时来找我。
t_en: Alright then, feel free to come find me anytime if you have any questions.

t_ch: 没什么想问的。
t_en: That's all I wanted to know.

t_ch: 那好，等你有什么问题了可以随时来找我。
t_en: Alright then, feel free to come find me anytime if you have any questions.

t_ch: 呵呵…年轻人…在这里许下心愿，问出问题，一切都有机会被解答……
t_en: Hehe... young one... Make a wish here, ask your questions, and all shall have a chance to be answered...

t_ch: 什么都能回答吗？
t_en: Can you answer anything?

t_ch: 年轻人，看你的问题，想必是初入绘世学院吧？我乃绘世学院诸多学员念力之化身，同学们都尊称我为「前辈」。
t_en: Young one, judging by your questions, you must be new to Graphia Academy, right? I am a psychic manifestation of the many students here at Graphia Academy. Everyone respectfully calls me their "senior."

t_ch: 我知晓这世上的所有答案，甚至包括你最想知道的考试答案。你要听选择题、计算题、还是主观题？
t_en: I know all the answers in this world, including those answers in exams you're dying to know. What'll it be... Multiple choice, calculation, or subjective question?

t_ch: 选择题答案是什么？
t_en: What's the answer to the multiple choice?

t_ch: 表达了作者的思乡之情。
t_en: Expresses the author's homesickness.

t_ch: 计算题答案是什么？
t_en: What's the answer to this calculation?

t_ch: C、B、A、A、C。
t_en: C, B, A, A, C.

t_ch: 主观题答案是什么？
t_en: What's the answer to the subjective question?

t_ch: 生命、宇宙和万物的终极答案。
t_en: The answer to life, the universe, and everything.

t_ch: 钝角。
t_en: Obtuse.

t_ch: 如何？是不是发现即使得到了这答案，你却依然很迷茫？因为你只知道答案，却不知道是哪场考试的答案。
t_en: Well? Even after getting the answer, you're still lost, aren't you? Because you've got the answers, but you don't know which exam they're for.

t_ch: 人生也是同样的道理。重要的从来不是写下「正确的答案」，而是找到「正确的问题」。
t_en: Life follows the same principle. What matters has never been about writing down "the right answer," but finding "the right question."

t_ch: 那正确的问题是什么？
t_en: So what's the right question?

t_ch: 「这学期我为什么没好好上课，以至于要跑来问一个雕像考试答案？」
t_en: "Why didn't I pay attention in class this semester, and now I'm here asking a statue for answers?"

t_ch: …但我一般不会这么回答，因为会被恼羞成怒的学生半夜泼油漆。好在你看起来不是那种坏学生，就把这个问题当作前辈的忠告记在心里吧。
t_en: ...but I usually don't answer like that, because I'd get tainted in the middle of the night by exasperated students. Fortunately, you don't seem like that kind of troublemaker, so just remember this question as my advice..

t_ch: 并没有什么想问的。
t_en: Nothing to ask.

t_ch: 那就继续前进吧，你会找到属于自己的「问题」和「答案」。
t_en: Then let's keep moving forward. You'll find your own "questions" and "answers."

t_ch: 你好吖，同学，你有钱吗？
t_en: Hey there, buddy! Got any cash on ya?

t_ch: 要钱没有要命一条
t_en: I've got nothing but my life

t_ch: 这就对了！啊…喵喵是想说，同学缺信用点的话，喵喵可以送你点信用点帮助你！
t_en: That's more like it! Ah... what I meant to say is, if you're running low on credits, I can give you some to help you out!

t_ch: 还有此等好事？
t_en: Is there really such a good deal?

t_ch: 那都是因为同学遇上了我这只猫美心善的送财喵呀！
t_en: That's because you ran into me, a kind-hearted Fortune Cat who brings fortune, meow!

t_ch: 不会有诈吧！
t_en: Is this some kind of scam!?

t_ch: 怎么会呢？你看我在学校里光明正大待了这么久，肯定不是坏人啦！
t_en: How could that be? Look, I've been hanging around school in broad daylight for so long. I'm definitely not a bad guy!

t_ch: 送财喵可是星际和平公司旗下的合法项目，喵~
t_en: The Fortune Cat Project is a legit operation under Interastral Peace Corporation, meow~

t_ch: 你看看，三年二班的切斯特顿同学每天穿着名牌衣服，游泳社的艾拉拿着月巴克，连刚入校的新生卡托都已完成寰宇旅游了！
t_en: Look at that! Chesterton from Year 3 Class 2 wears designer clothes every day, Ella from the Swim Club is always carrying Moonbucks, and even Catho, who just started here, has already gone on a universal tour!

t_ch: 看看他们，再看看自己，会不会觉得这样的学生生涯才是完整的？但没有信用点怎么完整呢？
t_en: Look at them, then look at yourself. Don't you think this is what a complete student life should be like? But how can it be complete without credits?

t_ch: 所以，送财喵的所作所为都是为了让同学们都能渡过充实有意义的学生生涯！
t_en: So, everything I do is to ensure that all students can have a fulfilling and meaningful school life!

t_ch: 辛苦学习为了什么？是为了考上好学校。考上学校是为了什么？是为了好工作。好工作是为了赚钱，赚钱是为了享受。
t_en: Why do you study so hard? To get into a good school. Why do you get into a school? For a good job. With a good job, you can make a lot of money for enjoyment.

t_ch: 现在喵喵就可以直接让你跳过这些漫长的阶段，直接享乐，这就是人生的终极奥义呀。
t_en: Now I can help you skip all these long phases and jump straight to the fun part. That's the ultimate secret of life!

t_ch: 这不就是校园贷？
t_en: Isn't this just a student loan scam?

t_ch: 不不不，这绝对不是，我们要拒绝校园贷！
t_en: No, no, no! Absolutely not! We have to say NO to student loans!

t_ch: 不，我要自己努力！
t_en: No, I'll work hard on my own!

t_ch: 喵喵喵，这位同学怎么就如此想不开呢？
t_en: Meow, meow, meow, what's gotten into your head?

t_ch: 这样吧，喵喵给你点钱，同学先去好好自我投资，如何？你想呀，身价上去了，自然有钱还给喵喵了，对不对？
t_en: Here's the deal. I'll spot you some money, and you can invest in yourself first. How's that sound? Think about it... Once your net worth rises, you'll easily get the money to pay me back, right?

t_ch: 还不上怎么办？
t_en: What if I can't pay it back?

t_ch: 没关系的同学，同学只需要把你的隐私权、肖像权、财产权、知识权、投票权、人身安全授权给喵喵就好啦！
t_en: No worries, dear student! Just sign over your privacy rights, image rights, property rights, intellectual rights, voting rights, and personal safety to me, and we're good!

t_ch: 太好了，快给我办理！
t_en: Great! Sign me up right away!

t_ch: 好的！同学，这边麻烦授权一下你的隐私权、肖像权、财产权、知识权、投票权以及人身安全。
t_en: Alright! Well, if you could authorize your privacy rights, image rights, property rights, intellectual rights, voting rights, and personal safety here, that'd be great.

t_ch: 正在办理中…喵喵喵？？？同学你不是二相乐园人吧，不是也没关系，正在查询你的个人信息……
t_en: Processing... What??? You're not from Planarcadia, are you? No worries if you're not. Let me just check your personal information...

t_ch: 提示：处理中…已查询到个人信息…{NICKNAME}…等等，{NICKNAME}？？？
t_en: Notice: Processing... Personal information found... {NICKNAME}... Wait, {NICKNAME}???

t_ch: 对…对…对不起…{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，我刚才跟您开玩笑呢喵！怎么会给您放贷呢喵！
t_en: S—Sorry, {NICKNAME}, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}, I was just kidding with you! As if I'd ever give you a loan, meow!

t_ch: 这种小玩笑，应该没必要告诉您那几位熟人吧喵？那啥，送财喵就要进入休眠时间咯！祝您生活顺利、事业有成喵！晚安喵！
t_en: No need to tell your friends about our little joke, meow? Well, then, this Fortune Cat is hitting the hay! Here's to your success and good fortune, meow! Good night, meow!

t_ch: 送财喵猝不及防地关上了自己的屏幕，开始竭尽全力地装死。
t_en: The Fortune Cat abruptly shuts off its screen and starts playing dead with all its might.

t_ch: 别想骗我，再见！
t_en: Don't try to fool me, goodbye!

t_ch: 提示：客户拒绝销售服务。您生活顺利、事业有成，再见喵。
t_en: Note: Customer declined sales service. Wish you a smooth life and success in your career. Goodbye, meow.

t_ch: 星际和平公司提醒您：理性消费、健康生活，远离校园贷。
t_en: The Interastral Peace Corporation reminds you: Spend responsibly, live healthily, and steer clear of predatory student loans.

t_ch: 啊…请稍等，我回复一下评论区的留言。好了——如您所见，我是这所艺术学校里的一名数学老师。如果你有需要消化的悲伤，可以把它们留给我…毕竟数学课本总是非常悲伤。
t_en: Ah... just a moment, let me reply to some comments in the section. There we go. As you can see, I'm a math teacher at this art school. If you have any sadness that needs processing, you can leave it with me... after all, math textbooks are always full of sorrow.

t_ch: 为什么数学课本总是悲伤？
t_en: Why are math textbooks always full of sorrow?

t_ch: 因为数学课本有太多「问题」，所以它总是非常悲伤。
t_en: This is because there are too many problems to solve in the textbooks.

t_ch: 不用，我天天开心。
t_en: No need, I'm quite happy with myself every day.

t_ch: 那太好了。数学课本有太多「问题」，所以它总是非常悲伤。
t_en: That's great! The reason why the math textbooks are always full of sorrow is that there are too many problems to solve.

t_ch: 我的悲伤没法与你分享。
t_en: I can't share my sorrow with you.

t_ch: 哦，这真不幸。我的朋友数学课本也是这样——它有太多「问题」，所以它总是非常悲伤。
t_en: Oh, that's unfortunate. My friend's math textbook feels just the same: It has too many problems to solve, and that has made it full of sorrow.

t_ch: 啊，一个小玩笑而已，不用在意。如果你有任何问题，欢迎向我提问——现在不是科研时间，而回答问题也正是老师应该承担的义务。
t_en: Ah, just a little joke, don't mind it. If you have any questions, feel free to ask me. It's not research time right now, and answering questions is precisely what a teacher's duty entails.

t_ch: 啊…评论区还是一如既往的幽默。如果你有任何问题，欢迎向我提问——现在不是科研时间。
t_en: Ah... the comment section is as humorous as ever. If you have any questions, feel free to ask me. It's not research time right now.

t_ch: 评论区是怎么回事？
t_en: What's going on in the comments section?

t_ch: 哦，我有一栏定期更新的播客栏目，叫做《泰科老师聊宇宙数学》。瞧，一百多万的订阅量，数据相当不错，评论区也很热闹。
t_en: Oh, I have a regularly updated podcast series called Mr. Tyker Talks Interstellar Math. Look, over a million subscribers! Got some pretty impressive stats, and the comments section is buzzing!

t_ch: 原来有这么多人喜欢数学。
t_en: I had no idea so many people were into math.

t_ch: 我很想这么说…可惜，真相并不是这样。
t_en: I wish I could say that... but unfortunately, that's not how things really are.

t_ch: 哇，竟然是金牌网红！
t_en: Wow, some top-tier influencer you are!

t_ch: 惭愧惭愧，一点虚名罢了。
t_en: Aw, thank you. You flatter me.

t_ch: 你究竟怎么办到的？
t_en: How on earth did you manage to do that?

t_ch: 说实话，我也感到很意外。
t_en: To be honest, I was pretty surprised, too.

t_ch: 一开始，我只是为了额外赚点愿力，把一些学术界的前沿成果做成视频发布到了网上。很长一段时间，这个播客都只有几千的订阅量…学生们的艺术作业，比它的播放数据可好多了。
t_en: At first, I was just trying to earn some extra Wishpower by turning cutting-edge academic findings into videos online. For a long time, this podcast only had a few thousand subscribers... My students' art assignments got way better view counts than that.

t_ch: 直到有一天，可能是为了获得一些行为艺术方面的节目效果吧…那位火花花小姐在直播中观看了我的一个视频。本来这也没什么，那天她看了很多奇奇怪怪的小众节目——
t_en: Until one day, probably for some performance art effect or something... that Miss Sparxicle watched one of my videos during her livestream. It wasn't really a big deal at first, since she had been watching all sorts of weird niche content that day...

t_ch: ——但好巧不巧，那一天，她刚好在看我的节目的时候，睡着了。
t_en: ...But as luck would have it, she happened to fall asleep while watching my show on that day.

t_ch: 火花花在直播的时候睡着？
t_en: Sparxicle fell asleep during her livestream?

t_ch: 懂了，你莫名其妙就火了。
t_en: I see. That's why you just randomly blew up and went viral.

t_ch: 嗯。我不知道这是不是她故意营造的节目效果…反正当天晚上，「火花花因为这个视频当场睡着」就上了热搜。大家纷纷留言，说我的声音治好了他们多年未愈的失眠……
t_en: Hmm. I can't tell if she meant to do that for the drama... but that night, "Sparxicle Falls Asleep During This Video" started trending. Everyone was commenting that my voice had cured their years of insomnia...

t_ch: 嗐。如果能从这个角度帮到更多的人，倒也不是一件坏事吧。
t_en: Heh. Well, if I can help more people in this way, I guess that's not such a bad thing.

t_ch: 宇宙际几何学是什么？
t_en: What is Interstellar Geometry?

t_ch: 数学的一个分支。
t_en: A branch of mathematics.

t_ch: 呃…没了？
t_en: Uh... That's it?

t_ch: 我上次尝试解释自己具体的研究领域的时候，那个对此好奇的学生当着我的面直接睡着了。在白天站着睡觉并不安全，所以我决定大幅简化这方面的自我介绍。
t_en: Last time I tried to explain my specific research field, one curious student fell asleep right in front of me. Since falling asleep while standing during the day isn't exactly safe, I decided to drastically simplify my self-introduction in this area.

t_ch: 那算了，就这样吧。
t_en: Forget it, let's just leave it at that.

t_ch: 我不信。我要满足好奇心。
t_en: I don't buy it. I wanna satisfy my curiosity.

t_ch: …好吧。
t_en: If you insist...

t_ch: 宇宙际几何学是研究乘法和加法间相互关系的学科。数学中有许多规则由乘法支配，也有许多规则由加法支配，乘法和加法之间虽然具备一定的转换关系，但很难利用这种关系研究更复杂的结构。
t_en: Interstellar Geometry is a discipline that studies the relationship between multiplication and addition. In mathematics, many rules are dominated by multiplication, and others by addition. Although there is a certain conversion relationship between multiplication and addition, it is difficult to use this relationship to study more complex structures.

t_ch: 因此我们将现实宇宙一分为二，一个由乘法支配，一个由加法支配，我们可以在这两个宇宙中分别建立一系列的████剧院，它们具有相同的全局域结构，可以通过名为宇航员的关系传递数据。
t_en: Therefore, we split the real universe into two: One dominated by multiplication and one by addition. We can establish a series of ████ theaters in these two universes, which share the same global domain structure and can transmit data through a relationship called astronauts.

t_ch: ████和████并不兼容，举例来说宇航员必须先破坏██数据在████中的对称性，仅保留它们在████的对称性，然后带着它们前往████。也就是说只能传递扭曲的██。
t_en: ████ and ████ are not compatible. For example, astronauts must first destroy the symmetry of ██ data in ████, only preserving their symmetry in ████, before taking them to ████. In other words, only distorted ██ can be transmitted.

t_ch: 但巧妙的是，通过██的编码，███携带的扭曲██可以在█████当中进行██的测量，并评估它在████当中的██。这种██就是宇宙际几何学的████。
t_en: However, cleverly, through ██ encoding, the distorted ██ carried by ███ can be ██ measured within █████, and its ██ in ████ can be evaluated. This ██ is the ████ of Interstellar Geometry.

t_ch: …你再也无法抵抗自己的睡意。
t_en: ...You can no longer resist your drowsiness.

t_ch: 当你醒来的时候，你发现自己似乎梦游了一段距离。
t_en: When you wake up, you find that you seem to have sleepwalked quite a distance.

t_ch: 在这里方便做科研吗？
t_en: Is this a good place for research?

t_ch: 人们都说，「钱多、事少、离家近」是工作的黄金标准…从这些角度来看，我现在的工作环境，毫无疑问非常理想。
t_en: You know what they say, "high pay, low workload, close to home" is the golden standard for a perfect job... Looking at it from these angles, my current work situation is, without a doubt, absolutely ideal.

t_ch: 虽然在一个艺术学校里任教会失去很多参与银河学术会议的机会，但宇宙际几何学本来就是个冷门学问，在很多人看来它既不宇宙也不几何…所以，清净也好。
t_en: Teaching at an art school means missing out on a lot of cosmic academic conferences, but Interstellar Geometry is such a niche field anyway. Most people think it's neither interstellar nor geometry... So, I guess a peaceful life isn't half bad.

t_ch: 老师再见。
t_en: See you, teach.
